title: Generic Module Profiles
goal: Implement #490 by replacing first-party feature-tier ontology with generic module profiles while preserving existing preset ergonomics.
non_goals: Do not remove planning or memory.
Do not build a plugin marketplace.
Do not make mutating lifecycle adapters generic in this slice.

intent.outcome: The registry treats planning and memory as bundled modules selected by generic profiles; feature tiers remain only as compatibility output.
intent.constraints: Preserve public preset behavior and keep lifecycle safety unchanged.
intent.latitude: Add canonical registry fields, compatibility aliases, README wording, and focused tests.
intent.escalation: Stop before third-party loading or lifecycle adapter redesign.

execution.next_step: Archive this plan, commit, close #490, refresh external evidence, then continue #485 with #510.
execution.proof: Run contract tooling, focused modules/preset tests, full workspace CLI tests, ruff, proof selector, and planning closeout.

invariants: Preset names and selected module behavior remain unchanged.
Feature tiers are no longer the canonical registry concept.
Routing-only remains absence of selected modules plus root routing surfaces.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Keep the root orchestrator thin, boring, generic, and strictly bounded.

required_continuation.activation trigger: After #490 closes, reingest and promote the next module-boundary-compression child.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Later MCP-style module component work can build on module profiles instead of feature tiers.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: Third-party discovery, MCP adapter boundary, structured-result tool metadata, and lifecycle adapter genericization.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: module_profiles are schema-backed, emitted by modules, used for preset expansion, and feature_tier output is marked compatibility.
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: Archive plan and commit after closeout checks.

intent_interpretation.literal request: Replace first-party feature tiers with generic module profiles and manifests.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Stop making planning/memory/full the orchestrator ontology while preserving convenient installer presets.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Add canonical module_profiles, derive preset/module selection from profiles, and keep feature_tiers as deprecated compatibility output.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Review whether compatibility output is acceptable until downstream report keys migrate.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: README, module registry contract/schema, CLI registry/profile selection, focused tests, planning surfaces.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: contract tooling, focused modules/preset tests, full workspace CLI tests, ruff, proof selector, planning checks.
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Stop before adding third-party module loading or changing lifecycle behavior.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: pyproject dependencies, generated packages, mutating lifecycle adapter internals, MCP runtime dependency.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Make generic module profiles the canonical selection model while preserving existing presets.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Do not break preset behavior or lifecycle safety.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Compatibility field names, schema wording, and test assertions.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: The change requires third-party loading, lifecycle genericization, or removal of public compatibility fields.

stop_conditions.stop when: Generic module profiles are canonical and behavior-compatible.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: The change requires removing public feature_tier/report fields immediately.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: The work becomes plugin discovery or lifecycle adapter genericization.

active_milestone.scope: Make module profiles canonical while preserving preset behavior.
active_milestone.ready: #490 is the selected next child for #485.

immediate_next_action: Add module_profiles to the registry/schema and use them in CLI output and preset selection.
validation_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q -k "modules_command_lists or selected_modules_uses_descriptor_owned_presets or preset_conflicts"
uv run ruff check src/agentic_workspace/cli.py tests/test_workspace_cli.py
uv run ruff check src tests

uv run agentic-workspace proof --target . --changed README.md src/agentic_workspace/cli.py src/agentic_workspace/contracts/module_registry.json src/agentic_workspace/contracts/schemas/module_registry.schema.json tests/test_workspace_cli.py .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml .agentic-workspace/planning/execplans/generic-module-profiles.plan.json --format json
uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json
uv run agentic-workspace report --target . --section closeout_trust --format json
uv run agentic-workspace defaults --section root_cli_authority --format json
completion_criteria: The module registry has schema-backed module_profiles.
Presets compile to selected module ids from module profile data.
Feature tiers are compatibility output, not canonical registry authority.
Planning and memory remain bundled first-party examples of the module contract.

execution_run.what happened: Added schema-backed module_profiles to the module registry, used profiles for preset expansion, emitted module_profiles from the modules command, and marked feature_tier report output as a deprecated compatibility alias.
execution_run.scope touched: README, module registry contract/schema, CLI module profile/preset handling, focused tests, planning closeout.
execution_run.validations run: focused module/preset tests, full workspace CLI tests, ruff, contract tooling, proof selector, root CLI authority query, planning checks.
execution_run.next step: Archive this plan, commit, close #490, refresh external evidence, then continue the module-boundary-compression lane.

finished_run_review.scope respected: yes; no lifecycle mutation, dependency, or third-party loading behavior changed.
finished_run_review.proof status: passed local validation and proof selection.
finished_run_review.intent served: yes; module profiles are canonical registry data and presets expand from profile selections.
finished_run_review.config compliance: used package summary/report/proof/doctor/defaults workflow and checked-in plan closeout.
finished_run_review.misinterpretation risk: medium-low; feature_tier compatibility fields remain intentionally until downstream surfaces migrate.
finished_run_review.follow-on decision: archive this slice but keep #485 open through #510-#514.

proof_report.validation proof: Local checks pass for focused module/preset behavior, full workspace CLI tests, ruff, contract tooling, proof selector, and planning doctor.
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: modules output exposes module_profiles and compatibility metadata; preset selection uses module profile selected_modules; full report marks feature_tier as a deprecated alias.

intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: The registry has canonical module_profiles, first-party presets compile from profile selected_modules, and feature_tier ontology is demoted to compatibility output.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Root orchestrator should be generic and bounded; first-party modules should be examples, not ontology.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Prefer compatibility-preserving contract migration over breaking public fields.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Does this make #510 MCP-style component alignment easier?

closure_check.why this decision is honest: The bounded slice is complete and remaining intent is routed to a checked-in continuation owner.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: Prepared closeout records intent satisfaction, closure decision, proof evidence, and distillation buckets.
closure_check.reopen trigger: Reopen when .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml roadmap lane module-boundary-compression activates a fresh bounded slice.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: Generic module profiles are schema-backed and emitted; feature tiers are compatibility output.
execution_summary.validation confirmed: Focused and full CLI tests plus ruff, contract tooling, proof selector, and planning checks passed.
execution_summary.post-work posterity capture: Archived execplan plus module_registry.json schema-backed contract metadata.
execution_summary.knowledge promoted (Memory/Docs/Config): Durable product decision lives in module_registry.json and README; no Memory update needed.
execution_summary.resume from: Reingest open issues and choose #510 after closing #490.

signals found: Initial execplan scaffold omitted delegated_judgment, which made active contract projections unavailable until corrected.
signals fixed: Added delegated_judgment before closeout; broader scaffold issue is already tracked by #515.

discard.summary: Command-by-command execution detail stays in the archived proof record.

memory.summary: Durable product decision lives in module_registry.json and README; no Memory update needed.
